parts.title: The Tryouts
parts.text: The morning of tryouts, **frost** still covered the field when Maya arrived, her breath making small clouds in the cold air. She had trained all winter in a **rustic** gym on the edge of town — bare wooden floors, no heating, just her and the work. Competing at the national level was a new **frontier** for her, and she gripped her water bottle and told herself she was ready.

parts.title: The Doubters
parts.text: A **series** of critics said Maya was too young and too unknown to belong in the competition. Coach Kai told her to **preserve** her energy for the field and ignore the noise. It was a **marvel** watching her train — she moved with speed and precision that seemed almost automatic, her body operating on pure **reflex**. She had discovered an **exotic** footwork technique from a training guide printed overseas, and it was unlike anything her opponents had seen.

parts.title: The Turning Point
parts.text: During the semifinal, Maya decided to **expose** a weakness she had spotted in the opposing team's defense. All week she had found time to **tinker** with her approach angle, making tiny adjustments, and now it paid off — she sprinted through a gap, tightened her **grip** on the ball, and scored the goal that changed the game. The crowd cheered so loudly it seemed to **revive** every tired muscle in her body.

parts.title: Victory
parts.text: They won the championship by a single point in the final seconds. Maya was lifted onto her teammates' shoulders. Kai told the reporters, "She's the hardest-working athlete I've ever coached. Everyone doubted her, but she proved them all wrong." That night, Maya looked at her medal and smiled. The underdog had become the champion.

definitions.meaning1: Unusual and exciting because it comes from a faraway place. Example: The zoo had exotic birds from tropical rainforests with bright feathers of every color.
definitions.studentChallenge: The market sold ___ fruits from distant countries that we had never tasted before.

definitions.meaning1: A thin layer of ice crystals that forms on surfaces when the temperature drops below freezing. Example: The frost on the window made beautiful, feathery patterns in the morning light.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ covered the grass like a sparkling white blanket on the cold November morning.

definitions.meaning1: The border of settled land; the edge of the unknown. Example: The pioneers headed west to the frontier.
definitions.meaning2: The limit of knowledge. Example: Space is the next frontier for exploration.
definitions.studentChallenge: Scientists are pushing the ___ of medical research.

definitions.meaning1: To be filled with wonder. Example: We marveled at the beautiful waterfall.
definitions.meaning2: Something amazing. Example: The new bridge is a marvel of engineering.
definitions.studentChallenge: The crowd ___ at the magician's tricks.

definitions.meaning1: An automatic body movement that happens without you thinking about it. Example: Pulling your hand away from a hot stove is a reflex that protects you from being burned.
definitions.studentChallenge: Blinking when something comes close to your eye is a natural ___ that helps keep you safe.

definitions.meaning1: To keep something safe or in good condition. Example: We need to preserve the rainforest.
definitions.studentChallenge: The museum works to ___ ancient artifacts.

definitions.meaning1: Relating to the countryside; simple and old-fashioned in a pleasant way. Example: The rustic wooden cabin had a stone fireplace, handmade furniture, and a view of the meadow.
definitions.studentChallenge: The family enjoyed their holiday in a ___ farmhouse surrounded by rolling green hills.

definitions.meaning1: A number of similar things or events that come one after another. Example: The library had a whole series of mystery books that she couldn't stop reading.
definitions.studentChallenge: We watched a ___ of short videos in class to learn about the water cycle.

definitions.meaning1: To make small changes or adjustments to try to fix or improve something. Example: Dad liked to tinker with old radios in the garage until they worked again.
definitions.studentChallenge: She loved to ___ with the engine of the go-kart to make it run faster.

definitions.meaning1: To make something visible or known. Example: The investigation exposed the truth.
definitions.studentChallenge: Don't ___ the film to direct sunlight.

definitions.meaning1: To hold something very tightly and firmly. Example: She had to grip the railing as the boat rocked back and forth in the waves.
definitions.meaning2: A firm, tight hold on something (noun). Example: He kept a strong grip on the leash so the dog would not run away.
definitions.studentChallenge: The rock climber used chalky hands to ___ the small ledge on the cliff.

definitions.meaning1: To bring back to life or make active again. Example: A glass of cold water revived her after the run.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The zoo had ___ birds from tropical rainforests — creatures with feathers of bright red, gold, and blue.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The market sold ___ fruits from faraway countries that none of us had ever seen or tasted before.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ on the window made feathery, lace-like patterns in the cold morning light.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The ___ covered the whole lawn like a sparkling white blanket on the freezing November morning.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The brave pioneers packed their wagons and headed west to the ___, into land nobody had mapped yet.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Scientists are constantly pushing the ___ of medical research, discovering things once thought impossible.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: We stood at the edge of the waterfall and could only ___ at the sheer power and beauty of it.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The crowd began to ___ at the magician's tricks — gasping and pointing in disbelief.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Pulling your hand away from a burning-hot stove is a ___ — your body moves before your brain even thinks.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Blinking when something flies close to your eye is a natural ___ that protects you from harm.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: We need to ___ the ancient rainforest before it disappears — once it is gone, it cannot be brought back.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The museum works hard every day to ___ rare artifacts so future generations can see and learn from them.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ wooden cabin had a stone fireplace, handmade chairs, and a sweeping view of the meadow.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The family loved their holiday in the ___ farmhouse, surrounded by rolling green hills and birdsong.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The library had a whole ___ of mystery books — twelve in total — and she could not stop reading them.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: We watched a ___ of short videos in class, one after another, to learn about the water cycle.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Dad loved to ___ with old radios in the garage on weekends, adjusting wires until they crackled to life.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: She liked to ___ with the go-kart engine, making tiny tweaks to squeeze out a little more speed.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The reporter worked for months to ___ the truth behind the strange disappearance of the old paintings.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Never ___ the film to direct sunlight — it will ruin all the photographs on the roll.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: She had to ___ the railing tightly as the ferry rocked and tilted in the rough, choppy waves.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The rock climber used chalk-covered hands to ___ the tiny ledge on the steep cliff face.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: A cold glass of water ___ her after the long, exhausting run in the summer heat.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The long-awaited rain finally came and began to ___ the wilting, dying plants in the garden.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage: Maya trained every morning in a small, _(1)_ gym — a simple, old-fashioned room with wooden floors and bare walls — that sat at the edge of a frozen field. In winter, _(2)_ covered the windows in delicate patterns, but Maya barely noticed; she was already stretching. Her coach had designed a _(3)_ of drills — one after another — that would test every part of her body and mind. Space exploration felt like the final _(4)_ of human ambition, but for Maya, the true unknown was what lay at the edge of her own limits. She wanted to _(5)_ everything she had achieved — to keep it safe and never let it slip away. She learned to _(6)_ in her own performance, adjusting her footwork by tiny amounts until each movement felt perfect. A sip of water was enough to _(7)_ her energy between the hardest drills. Her _(8)_ was lightning fast — she caught the ball before she even had time to think about catching it. She had discovered an _(9)_ training technique from a distant country, one that nobody else in her league had ever tried. Kai would watch and _(10)_ every time she attempted the difficult leap — filled with wonder and disbelief. She learned to _(11)_ the rope tightly and pull herself up without swinging, controlled and strong. "Never _(12)_ your weaknesses to the competition," Kai told her. "Keep them guessing until the very last second."

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
